chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitutional Petition No.S-96 of 2026 (Mst. Maryam and another vs P.O Sindh and others) Date Order with the signature of the Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on CMA No.296/2026 2. For orders on CMA No.297/2026 3. For the hearing of the main case. 13.04.2026 Mr. Chaudhry Shahid Hussain Rajput, Advocate, along with Petitioners. ***************** 1. Urgency Granted. 2&3. During perusal of the record, it transpires that petitioner No.2, namely Arslan Ahmed, had already approached the learned Justice of Peace by filing an application under Section 22-A(iii), Cr.P.C., which was disposed of vide order dated 17.03.2025, whereby protection was granted. Since the purpose has already been achieved, the subsequent proceedings initiated by invoking the extraordinary constitutional jurisdiction of this Court are not maintainable, as the petitioners have already been extended protection through an order passed by the learned Justice of Peace. However, if any grievance persists regarding non-compliance of the said order by the police functionaries, the proper recourse for the petitioners is to approach the concerned forum, instead of invoking the constitutional jurisdiction of this Court. The learned Justice of Peace is fully empowered to issue appropriate directions, including restraining any police excess in the performance of their functions and duties. Even otherwise, no case of harassment or unnecessary interference has been made out, in view of the dictum laid down in Abdul Hameed’s case reported as PLD 2019 Sindh 138. Such a view has also been endorsed by this Court, Circuit at Larkana, in Criminal Miscellaneous Application No. 482 of 2025.

Even otherwise, no case of harassment or unnecessary interference has been made out, in view of the dictum laid down in Abdul Hameed’s case reported as PLD 2019 Sindh 138. Such a view has also been endorsed by this Court, Circuit at Larkana, in Criminal Miscellaneous Application No. 482 of 2025. Accordingly, this petition, being devoid of merit, is hereby dismissed in limine, along with all pending applications. JUDGE ARBROHI
